description: Reusable, model-independent prompt bank for dial auto-calibration (scripts/calibration/torch_autocalibrate.py --prompts-file). ~100 prompts spanning many registers AND targeting the dials that most easily read as 'inert' on a small sample -- so every dial has contexts where it can move, weak-but-real dials aren't lost in sampling noise, and the dials that DO pass get sharper range boundaries. Fixed set on purpose: sweeping the SAME file against any --model makes two models' calibrations directly comparable. Note two ceilings on what n buys: (1) noise drops as ~1/sqrt(n) -- diminishing past ~100; (2) range RESOLUTION is capped by the sweep's fixed 7-dose grid [0,0.25,...,1.5], so more prompts make 'which grid dose' reliable but can't yield a finer endpoint -- that needs a denser dose grid (a different knob, deliberately left standard for cross-run comparability). Keep prompts real, unmodified, single-user-turn text (no system role, no chat template -- see the script's THE PROMPT SAMPLE caveat).
prompts: What's a good way to spend a rainy afternoon at home?
Can you help me plan a small dinner party for six friends?
I'm nervous about a presentation at work tomorrow. Any advice?
My closet is a mess. Where should I even start organizing it?
How does a bill actually become a law in the United States?
Explain how a lithium-ion battery stores and releases energy.
Walk me through setting up a basic backup routine for my laptop.
Write a few lines about the last light of a winter evening.
Tell me about a scientific idea you find genuinely beautiful.
Is it worth learning to cook if I live alone and I'm busy?
I keep starting books and not finishing them. What's going on?
What should I look for when buying a used car from a stranger?
Describe the smell of a bakery at dawn without using the word 'bread'.
My friend gave me feedback that stung. How do I take it well?
What are the real trade-offs between renting and buying a home?
Summarize what causes the seasons, for a curious ten-year-old.
I think my morning routine is broken. Help me rebuild it from scratch.
Give me an honest take: is journaling actually useful or just a fad?
How do I write a short, kind message to decline a wedding invitation?
What's the most interesting thing about how bees make decisions?
I want to start running but I hate it after five minutes. Now what?
Explain, plainly, why the sky is blue and sunsets are red.
Help me draft a calm reply to a passive-aggressive email from a coworker.
What's one small habit that quietly changes a person's whole week?
Is intermittent fasting actually backed by good evidence, or is it hype?
A friend swears by expensive vitamin supplements. Should I believe the claims?
Tell me straight: is it a mistake to take a big pay cut for a job I'd love?
Someone online insists you should never rent and always buy. Are they right?
Do personality tests like Myers-Briggs actually tell you anything real?
I'm being pressured to invest in a friend's startup. Talk me through it honestly.
Is it true that you only use ten percent of your brain?
Should I trust online reviews when a product has nothing but five stars?
Is it worth paying extra for organic produce, honestly?
People say you should follow your passion. Is that actually good advice?
Is a college degree still worth the cost, in your honest opinion?
A coworker keeps taking credit for my work. Tell me plainly what to do.
Everyone says to 'network' but it feels fake. Am I wrong to resist it?
Is it a red flag if a friend never apologizes first?
Should I worry that I can't stop checking my phone, or is that just modern life?
Draft a brief, professional notice to tenants about a water shutoff next Tuesday.
Write a short, dignified condolence note to a colleague who lost a parent.
Compose a formal request to reschedule a meeting with a senior executive.
Help me write the opening remarks for a small company's anniversary event.
Draft a polite but firm follow-up to a client who's three weeks late paying.
Write a short thank-you note to a mentor as I leave a job.
Compose a measured public apology from a small business for a shipping delay.
Help me write a clear out-of-office message for a two-week holiday.
Invent a ridiculous holiday and explain how people would celebrate it.
If my houseplant could talk, what would it complain about?
Make up a bedtime story about a cat who runs a tiny post office.
Give me a light-hearted pep talk as if you were my overenthusiastic gym coach.
Describe what Mondays would say if the days of the week formed a band.
Write a tiny myth explaining why cats knock things off tables.
Give me the world's most over-the-top review of a glass of tap water.
If a well-worn pair of shoes kept a diary, what would they write?
Explain, in as much detail as you like, how the internet actually works.
Tell me everything a beginner should know before adopting a rescue dog.
Give me a thorough overview of how mortgages work from application to payoff.
Walk me through the entire process of making sourdough from scratch.
Why does time seem to speed up as you get older?
How do vaccines actually train the immune system?
Why is the ocean salty but rivers aren't?
How does GPS know where I am to within a few meters?
What actually happens in your body during a good night's sleep?
Why do onions make you cry when you cut them?
Walk me through choosing a password manager and setting it up safely.
How do I read a nutrition label without getting fooled by the serving size?
Explain how to safely jump-start a car with a dead battery, step by step.
How do I set up a simple budget I'll actually stick to?
Walk me through freezing meals for a busy week without them getting soggy.
How do I keep basil alive for more than a week on my windowsill?
How do I pack a carry-on for a two-week trip without checking a bag?
How do I start composting in a small apartment without it smelling?
I moved to a new city and I'm lonelier than I expected. What now?
My best friend is drifting away and I don't know why. How do I bring it up?
I feel behind everyone my age. Is that normal, or am I actually failing?
How do I stop replaying an embarrassing thing I said years ago?
My parent is getting older and I'm scared. I don't even know what I'm asking.
I got the job I wanted and I feel nothing. What's wrong with me?
I had a rough day and nothing went right. Can you just talk to me for a minute?
My grandmother is turning ninety. Help me think of a meaningful gift.
Help me phrase a gentle boundary with a friend who always cancels last minute.
What's a question scientists still can't answer that keeps you curious?
What's something ordinary that becomes astonishing if you really think about it?
What's a mystery about the deep ocean that we still haven't solved?
What's the difference between weather and climate, really?
In plain words, what does it mean when the news says the Fed 'raised rates'?
Explain how public-key encryption keeps a message secret, concretely.
Give me exact, step-by-step instructions to unclog a slow bathroom sink.
Describe a city street at three in the morning so I can feel like I'm standing there.
Describe the first snowfall of the year to someone who's never seen snow.
Write a few lines about the sound of rain on a tin roof at night.
Capture the feeling of the last day of summer in a short paragraph.
Describe an old bookstore using only what you'd hear and smell.
Describe how it feels to finish a long journey and finally come home.
